what_it_does: Defines a stroke-only SVG icon showing three circular nodes connected by two diagonal line segments, representing a share or network action.
observations: The SVG uses a 24 by 24 viewBox with no fixed width or height.
Rendering is stroke-based with fill set to none and stroke set to currentColor.
It draws three circles at left-center, upper-right, and lower-right positions.
It connects the left node to each right-side node with two path line segments.
standouts: Uses currentColor for inherited icon color.
Uses round stroke caps and joins with 1.5 stroke width.
conventions: Follows common inline SVG icon conventions with xmlns, viewBox, currentColor, and stroke-linecap/stroke-linejoin attributes.
Uses explicit circle elements for nodes and a path for connectors.
Does not include title, desc, role, or aria attributes inside the SVG.
concerns: No embedded accessibility metadata is present in the SVG file itself.
notes: A reusable share-network icon made from three circular nodes and two connector strokes.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consuming component or embedded in the SVG?
Is the 1.5 stroke width the intended icon stroke standard for this icon set?
purpose_vs_actual: The file name indicates a share-network icon, and the SVG content matches that purpose with connected nodes.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a scalable, theme-inheriting share/network icon for a design system or UI asset set.
contribution: Contributes a compact currentColor SVG share/network glyph with three nodes and two connector strokes that can be retained as a reusable icon asset in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 stroke-only currentColor SVG share-network icon composed of three circular nodes connected by two diagonal strokes.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing sharing, branching, or network relationships as a reusable design-system icon.
